executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has escalated significantly with confirmed death of Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei, first US casualties (3 troops killed), and expansion of hostilities into new theaters. The 24h incident count reached 790 (355 Major), with the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed and Iranian strikes now directly targeting Israeli cities, British military assets in Cyprus, and Gulf infrastructure. UK authorized US use of British bases for strikes on Iran, marking direct European involvement.
whats_new: UK Prime Minister Starmer confirms US can use British bases for missile strikes on Iran, marking direct European military involvement
US accelerates war on Iran's missile capabilities amid concerns over depleting US interceptor missile reserves
RAF responding to suspected Iranian drone strike at RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus - first Iranian attack on UK territory
Israeli airstrike on girls school in Minab, Iran kills approximately 180 children - confirmed by UNESCO
Israeli missiles strike Tehran's Niloofar Square killing over 20 civilians including children at a school
IRGC claims to have struck US aircraft carrier Abraham Lincoln, causing its withdrawal toward Indian Ocean
Bahrain reports Iranian attack on offshore facility near Salman Port - escalating attacks on Gulf allies
Israel issues evacuation warnings for ~50 Lebanese settlements, indicating preparations for major ground operation
Pro-Iran protests in Pakistan: 10 killed trying to storm US consulate in Karachi
confirmed_events: Khamenei confirmed dead - joint US-Israeli strike on Iran, Tehran officially declared 40-day mourning
CENTCOM confirms 3 US service members killed, 5 wounded in Iran's retaliatory strikes on US bases in Iraq/Syria region
Bahrain confirms downing 61 ballistic missiles and 34 Iranian drones targeting US 5th Fleet base
Hezbollah formally entered the conflict, launching precision missiles and drones at Israeli missile-defense site near Haifa
Oil prices surge 13% to $82/barrel (14-month high) amid Strait of Hormuz closure fears
Dubai and Abu Dhabi stock exchanges closed for two days due to ongoing hostilities
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims to have struck US aircraft carrier Abraham Lincoln causing withdrawal to Indian Ocean - UNVERIFIED, no independent confirmation
IRGC claims strikes on 3 US/UK oil tankers in Strait of Hormuz - UNVERIFIED, no coalition confirmation
IRGC claims latest waves of Op. True Promise 4 resulted in tanker strikes and base shutdowns with heavy casualties - UNVERIFIED scale

people_travel: Japan planning evacuation of citizens from Israel - first major third-party civilian evacuation
Israel issuing mass evacuation orders for ~50 Lebanese settlements - potential ground operation imminent
Pro-Iran protests spreading to Pakistan and India with casualties at US diplomatic facilities
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed - IRGC conducting tanker attacks, major global oil chokepoint paralyzed
Dubai and Abu Dhabi stock exchanges closed for two trading days - unprecedented market suspension
Bahrain Salman Port offshore facility attacked - key Gulf maritime infrastructure targeted
market_macro: Oil prices surge 13% to $82/barrel - 14-month high amid Hormuz disruption fears
Insurance premiums for Gulf shipping expected to skyrocket as conflict zones expand
Potential supply shock if Hormuz closure persists beyond 48-72h - watch for strategic petroleum reserve releases

outlook_24_72h.base_case: Conflict will continue escalating over the next 24-72h. US announced 'Operation Epic Rage' with intent to destroy Iranian military leadership and infrastructure. Trump warned war could last four weeks. Iran has demonstrated willingness to strike Gulf allies, Israeli cities, and now UK territory in Cyprus. IRGC proxy network (Hezbollah, Houthis, Iraqi militias) is fully mobilized. Expect continued Iranian ballistic missile barrages against Israel and US bases, potential tanker warfare in Gulf, and Israeli ground operations in Lebanon. Humanitarian crisis will worsen as civilian casualties mount.
escalation_risks: IRGC closing Strait of Hormuz - potential complete blockade would trigger global energy crisis
Iranian attacks on Gulf Arab infrastructure could drag Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain into direct conflict
Hezbollah-Israel ground war in Lebanon - evacuation orders suggest imminent Israeli invasion
UK involvement (bases for US strikes, drone attack on Cyprus) may draw British forces into direct combat
Iranian drone/missile campaign against US regional bases could expand to Jordan, Qatar, or Saudi facilities
Nuclear facility strikes - US targeting Iranian missile capabilities may inadvertently hit nuclear sites, risking wider escalation
Russia/China intervention - both have strategic interests in regional stability and may respond to protect Iran
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: No immediate de-escalation pathways visible. Iran has stated it will exercise 'no limit' to self-defense following Khamenei's death. Trump offered Iranian forces ultimatum to surrender weapons or face destruction - rejected by Tehran. US domestic political pressure mounting (poll shows Americans oppose war, Bernie Sanders criticizing involvement). Saudi Arabia urging neighbors not to escalate with Iran. However, with leadership真空 in Tehran, hardened IRGC commanders now in control, and Israeli war objectives focused on eliminating Iranian threat permanently, both sides appear committed to continued military action. Only intervention by Russia/China or catastrophic oil/supply shock could force mediation.
